Figure 6: MRC vs. LLM multi-agent baselines (Single seed (TLLM=0.7))
Figure 8: Shapley coalition ablation: individual agents, pairwise coalitions, grand coalition, and full MRC (Single seed (TLLM=0.7))
Figure 7: MRC vs. DRL baselines (Single seed (TLLM=0.7))
Figure 1: The general N -agent Online Shapley Cooperation mechanism.

Table 1: Main results over 1,037 trading days (2023-03-01 to 2025-12-31). Non-benchmark methods: mean± std (5 seeds); benchmarks: deterministic. Red: best mean per column among non-benchmark methods; Blue: second best. Superscripts on MRC denote two-sample t-test significance versus the second-best method per metric: ∗p<0.10, ∗∗p<0.05, ∗∗∗p<0.01 (Welch’s t-test, n=5 seeds each). The transaction cost is 0bps. For more cost sensitivity analysis, please refer to Table 8 (Appendix H).

Table 2: Shapley Council ablation: each coalition as a standalone decision maker (5 seeds, mean± std). Metrics as in Table 1. Superscript ∗∗ on MRC IR: two-sample t-test p<0.05 versus the secondbest method (Grand Coalition) on IR.

Table 11: LLM backbone ablation — Stage 3: Grand Coalition and Full MRC (2023-03-01–2025-12- 31, 1 037 days). The Stage 3 grand-coalition readout w123 is produced by A4 from the full coalition context. Market benchmarks have no grand-coalition counterpart (n/a). See Table 9 for Stage 1 and Table 10 for Stage 2 results. Red: best per column; Blue: second best.

Table 10: LLM backbone ablation — Stage 2: Pairwise Coalitions and Full MRC (2023-03-01– 2025-12-31, 1 037 days). P12: Market⊕On-chain Socratic debate; P13: Market⊕Macro debate; P23: On-chain⊕Macro debate. Market benchmarks have no pairwise-coalition counterpart (n/a). See Table 9 for Stage 1 and Table 11 for Stage 3 results. Red: best per column; Blue: second best.

Table 12: LLM backbone ablation — Inference cost and parse failure rate (2023-03-01–2025-12- 31, 1 037 days). Parse failure occurs when an LLM response cannot be decoded into a valid portfolio weight vector; the system falls back to the previous period’s weights for that step. Rates are computed over all 1 037 rebalancing steps. The end-to-end Full MRC failure rate (all three stages fail simultaneously) is 0.10% uniformly across all backbones. Inference time reports mean± std. per individual LLM call: Stage 1 = one specialist-agent call; Stage 2 = one pairwise-debate turn; Stage 3 = grand-coalition synthesis call; Full MRC = total wall-clock time per rebalancing period. Red: best (lowest) per column; Blue: second best. ↓ lower is better. See Tables 9–11 for portfolio performance metrics.
Figure 12: Full specification of A1 Market Observer (Alex Chen).
Figure 2: The MRC mechanism applied with N=3 agents for crypto portfolio management.
Figure 11: Cumulative returns and portfolio weights for all 17 strategies.
Figure 13: Full specification of A4 Investment Analyst (Director Morgan).

Table 9: LLM backbone ablation — Stage 1: Individual Agents and Full MRC (2023-03-01–2025- 12-31, 1 037 days). Each specialist agent operates independently without cross-agent communication. A1: Market Observer; A2: On-chain Observer; A3: Macro Observer. See Table 10 for Stage 2 and Table 11 for Stage 3 results. Red: best per column; Blue: second best.

Table 3: Regime-conditional performance decomposition (1,037 days). Bull / Bear / Volatile identified by ξ(t). Metrics as in Table 1. Red: best per column among MRC and LLM/DRL baselines (Shapley ablations and benchmarks excluded); Blue: second best. Single seed (TLLM=0.7).
Figure 10: Cumulative Hold returns of the 13 tokens and EW benchmark (2023-03-01 to 2025- 12-31). Each panel: single-asset cumulative return; final value annotated top-left.

Figure 9: Dynamic blend ratios and agent consensus (2023-03-01 to 2025-12-31). Top: Stage-1 blend β(t) S1 confirms that the Stage-1 ensemble dominates Stage-2 debate outputs across all regimes. Middle: consensus κ(t) (dashed line at 0.55 shown for visual reference), where lower consensus continuously reduces the grand-coalition readout blend via Eq. (10). Bottom: grand-coalition readout blend βfinal
Figure 4: Full-chain explainability under regime-dependent rebalancing.
Figure 3: Shapley weight evolution. Top: Bayesian weights ω(t) i for three agents. Middle: ρ(t)i for each Stage-1 individual coalition. Bottom: conditioning regime score ξ(t).
